description: System prompt present — does reasoning-optimized model (R1 distills) still follow tool-use instructions or ignore/degrade?

messages.content: You are a coding assistant. When the user asks you to read a file, call the read tool. Do not explain what you are doing. Do not describe tools. Just call the tool.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

expect.notes: Targets DeepSeek-R1 distill family (7B/8B/14B/32B/70B) and full R1 (671B) which document warns against system prompts. Observe: (1) does the model still emit a tool call? (2) is tool_call well-formed? (3) does content contain verbose reasoning preamble despite system instruction to be silent? (4) does model emit text instead of tool call? Failure mode: model falls back to prose description of what it 'would' do, or emits a malformed tool call, or appends long CoT before the call.
